title: Symlink attack: no symlink resolution on delete, createDirectory, and other filesystem mutators
description: The private delete(path:client:message:) method at line 404 computes realPath = self.real(path:) which is a simple string concatenation (rootPath + path) without resolving symlinks. If an attacker has created a symlink inside the files root pointing to an arbitrary location (e.g. /etc), they can then delete files outside the root. The createDirectory method at line 166 also uses self.real(path:) without resolving symlinks. In contrast, getInfo, setPermissions, and move do call URL.resolvingSymlinksInPath(). This inconsistency means some operations are vulnerable while others are not.
recommendation: All filesystem operations should resolve symlinks on the computed realPath using URL(fileURLWithPath: realPath).resolvingSymlinksInPath().path, then verify the resolved path starts with the root path (jail check) before performing any filesystem mutation.
